title: bartonia paniculata subsp. paniculata
intro: Bartonia paniculata subsp. paniculata, commonly known as the Yellow Bird or Grass-pink, is a perennial plant in the Gentianaceae family, native to North America. The plant's roots and underground stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bartonia paniculata subsp. paniculata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of health concerns, although further research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness.
